executive_summary: Crypto remittance unit economics are critically weak — LTV/CAC ratio of 0.29x with 42.7-month payback driven by low per-transaction ARPU ($8/mo) against consumer fintech CAC ($188), despite the transaction-fee revenue model being overwhelmingly validated by 6+ successful companies including public companies Remitly ($1.26B revenue) and Wise (£1.05B revenue).
score_reasoning: Score 34/100 — LTV/CAC Ratio: 2/25 (LTV $55 / CAC $188 = 0.29x; ARPU $8/mo from $300 avg tx × 2% fee × 1.33 tx/mo; churn 8%/mo from fintech payments benchmark; gross margin 55% from Remitly 59.3% benchmark adjusted for early-stage). Revenue Model Validation: 22/25 (6+ successful companies: Remitly public $1.26B rev, Wise public £1.05B rev, Western Union $4.05B rev, Bitso $331M funded $12B tx vol, Strike $90M funded, SukuPay embedded in Banco Industrial Guatemala; transaction-fee model is clearly dominant). Payback Period: 2/25 (42.7 months; CAC $188 / monthly margin $4.40; far exceeds 24-month ceiling). Pricing Power: 8/25 (pricing spans 0% to 10% of transfer amount >5x range but dominant free/near-free alternatives exist — Binance Pay 0%, Strike 0.99%, Valora gas-only; competitive pressure at low end constrains pricing for crypto entrants). Total: 2 + 22 + 2 + 8 = 34.

data.model_justification: All successful remittance competitors use transaction-fee models (percentage of transfer + FX spread). Remitly, Wise, Western Union, Strike, and Bitso all charge per-transaction fees. This is the universally dominant model in cross-border remittances — no subscription or freemium model has achieved meaningful scale in this segment.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Pain intensity is high (upstream score 78), justifying mid-to-high positioning in competitive range (0%-10%). At 2% total cost (fee + FX spread), positioned above crypto-native competitors (Strike 0.99%) but well below traditional MTOs (Western Union 6-10%), targeting the value-conscious but reliability-seeking migrant worker segment.

unit_economics.churn_source: BusinessDojo 2025 fintech retention benchmarks: payments apps 90-92% monthly retention (8-10% churn); crypto retail 85-92% retention. Used 8% as payments/crypto hybrid.
unit_economics.margin_source: Remitly gross profit margin 59.3% in FY2024 (finbox.com); adjusted down to 55% for early-stage crypto startup with lower scale efficiencies. Wise at 74%, Western Union at ~40-45%.
unit_economics.cac_source: First Page Sage 2026 Fintech CAC Benchmarks: Cryptocurrency consumer CAC = $188. B2C social media channel $212, PPC/SEM $290. Used $188 as blended consumer crypto fintech benchmark.

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $225.60 worsens an already unviable ratio to 0.24x with 51+ month payback — deepens the fundamental unit economics problem.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Churn increase to 9.6% reduces LTV to $45.83 and ratio to 0.24x — base case is already unviable so higher churn makes it worse but payback unchanged.

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to 1.6% of transfer drops ARPU to $6.40/mo and LTV to $44 with 0.23x ratio and 53-month payback — further compresses already negative economics.

model_precedents.evidence: Public company (NASDAQ: RELY), $1.264B revenue FY2024, 7.8M active customers, $54.6B send volume, approaching GAAP profitability in 2025
Public company (LSE: WISE), £1.052B revenue FY2024, 12.8M active customers, £481.4M profit before tax, £118.5B cross-border volume
$331M funding, $2.2B valuation, $12B+ transactions in 2024, 9M+ users, 10%+ of US-Mexico remittance corridor
$90M funding (Series B), $6B transaction volume, Lightning Network near-instant transfers

model_precedents.segment: Crypto-rail remittances US to Central America (Guatemala)
model_precedents.evidence: Embedded in Banco Industrial (Guatemala's largest bank) mobile app Zigi; $0.99 transfers in under 20 seconds; targeting $21B Guatemala remittance market
Public company, $4.05B revenue, 500K+ agent locations, dominant global brand, piloting stablecoin settlements

assumptions: Average transaction size of $300 based on upstream Problem data (Inter-American Dialogue: senders average $300/transaction 16x/year)
Transaction frequency of 1.33/month (16 transactions per year / 12 months) based on upstream Problem data
2% total cost (fee + FX spread) as price point, positioned mid-to-high in competitive range given high pain intensity
Monthly churn rate of 8% from BusinessDojo 2025 fintech payments benchmark (90-92% retention); conservative estimate for payments/crypto hybrid
Consumer crypto fintech CAC of $188 from First Page Sage 2026 benchmark report
Gross margin of 55% based on Remitly FY2024 (59.3%) adjusted down for early-stage scale inefficiencies; Wise at 74% represents upper bound
